stem: There are k apples and 9 oranges in a basket. If one of these pieces of fruit is selected at random, what is the probability of selecting an apple, in terms of k?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. The total number of pieces of fruit is k + 9, so the probability of selecting an apple is k/(k+9).

Choice A is incorrect; this is oranges divided by apples. Choice B is incorrect; this is apples divided by oranges. Choice C is incorrect; this is the probability of selecting an orange.
